meaning: adj.粗造的,喧闹的,到处闲荡的n.闹剧,小游艇,流浪汉
examples: The clowns gave a knockabout performance.
And this is all merely the warm-up - the legal knockabout before the trial itself can begin.
And then, again, there are the smaller feet of the boys and girls, mostly shod in knockabout sneakers.
He seems to belong to a different play, one of long, anguished soliloquies interspersed with occasional knockabout intermissions with great sports figures of the past.
